architecture.summary: EHRAgent is an LLM agent that generates and executes code to query electronic health records. The system injects medical domain knowledge into the LLM prompt, uses the LLM to generate Python code that decomposes complex tabular reasoning tasks into sequences of database queries and transformations, executes that code against real EHR databases, captures execution errors and feedback, and iteratively refines the code through error-driven in-context learning until the query succeeds.

biblio.title: EHRAgent: Code Empowers Large Language Models for Few-shot Complex Tabular Reasoning on Electronic Health Records

claims.statement: EHRAgent outperforms the strongest baseline by up to 29.6% in success rate
EHRAgent enables autonomous code generation and execution to facilitate clinicians in directly interacting with EHRs using natural language
EHRAgent formulates multi-tabular reasoning task based on EHRs as a tool-use planning process, efficiently decomposing a complex task into a sequence of manageable actions with external toolsets
By integrating interactive coding and execution feedback, EHRAgent effectively learns from error messages and iteratively improves its originally generated code
EHRAgent achieves strong capacity to tackle complex clinical tasks with minimal demonstrations
